Blueprint engine reads this for deterministic nodes and agent tool access.","status":"hooked","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"bca/polecats/furiosa","owner":"github@barti.mozmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T21:07:48Z","created_by":"Monsieur Barti","updated_at":"2026-03-04T21:12:39Z"}

new_value:
{"design":"DESIGN: Project Profile Schema

## Architecture

Single module: `src/profile/` — schema definition, loader, and types.

### Config File: `.pi/pipeline.yaml`

```yaml
project:
  language: typescript          # Language identifier
  packageManager: bun           # Package manager (bun, npm, pnpm, yarn, pip, cargo)

commands:
  install: bun install          # Dependency installation
  lint: bun run lint            # Linting command
  test: bun test                # Test runner
  build: bun run build          # Build command
  format: bun run format        # Formatter (optional)
  typecheck: bun run typecheck  # Type checking (optional)

tools:                          # Curated agent tool list
  - read
  - write
  - edit
  - bash
  - grep
  - glob

rules:                          # Paths to rules/context files
  - CLAUDE.md
  - .cursorrules

git:
  baseBranch: main              # PR target branch
  commitPrefix: ""              # Optional commit message prefix

pr:
  template: .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md  # Optional PR template path
```

### Implementation

1. `src/profile/schema.ts` — Zod schema + TS types
2. `src/profile/loader.ts` — YAML loader with defaults + validation
3. `src/profile/index.ts` — Public re-exports

### Principles
- Sensible defaults: works with zero config
- Strict validation: fail fast on bad config
- Extensible: easy to add new fields later
- No external deps beyond zod and yaml parser"}

{"description":"attached_molecule: bca-wisp-srjq
attached_formula: shiny
attached_at: 2026-03-04T21:27:37Z
attached_args: Use TypeScript with Bun. A project profile schema already exists in src/profile/ - import and use it. The engine should: 1) Parse YAML blueprint files defining nodes with type (deterministic|agent), dependencies, and commands. 2) Execute nodes in topological order respecting deps. 3) Deterministic nodes run shell commands directly. 4) Agent nodes will later invoke Pi SDK (stub for now). 5) Handle success/failure transitions. Keep it minimal.

This runner: 1) Takes a task description + project profile path + blueprint path as input. 2) Creates a Pi agent session using @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent SDK createAgentSession(). 3) Feeds the task as a prompt. 4) Handles completion and returns exit code. Check Pi SDK docs: the key function is createAgentSession() with SessionManager.inMemory() and session.prompt(). Keep it minimal.

{"id":"bca-3lt","title":"[bca] CI + hooks","description":"Je veux qu’on mette des hooks Git, pour que, avant chaque commit, on vérifie que les tests passent et que les builds réussissent. Je veux aussi un hook avant le push pour s’assurer que toute la CI passe. Ensuite, créons une CI complète qui vérifie que l’application fonctionne : tests, linter et tout le reste. Je veux vraiment un système bien complet pour être sûr que, lorsqu’une pull request est ouverte, le code ajouté ne casse rien et que tout fonctionne comme avant et que les fonctionnalites ajoutees marchent bien. L'idée, c'est que quand la pipeline tourne et qu'on a une PR, cette PR soit le plus possible en accord avec les principes de code qu'on a et qu'elle soit 100 % fonctionnelle. On se contente de reviewer le code, de faire un ou deux retours, et voilà.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@barti.mozmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:41:14Z","created_by":"Monsieur Barti","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:41:14Z"}

On se contente de reviewer le code, de faire un ou deux retours, et voilà."}
